sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jacqueline, presented with anxiety about her recent blood work showing hepatitis C.
She reports feeling fine despite the diagnosis and mentions that she was never previously told she had hepatitis C.
She has a history of IV drug use in the past, which she has been clean from for over 15 years.
She also reports a history of heavy alcohol use, currently consuming about one beer per day, and smoking one to two cigarettes per day.
She denies any other medical conditions, but mentions feeling tired at the end of the day despite getting enough sleep.
Her family history includes hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of IV drug use, heavy alcohol use, and smoking.
She also has a family history of hypertension, diabetes, and depression.
Review of systems: The patient reports feeling tired at the end of the day, but denies any other symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient appears in no distress and no jaundice on skin exam.
The heart exam reveals a regular rate and rhythm with a 2/6 systolic murmur, which has not changed from last year.
The lungs are clear with no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.
The abdomen is soft with no hepatosplenomegaly.
The musculoskeletal exam is normal with no gait disturbance or edema.
Test Results: The patient's recent blood work shows a positive HCV antibody test, an elevated AST at 39, and normal albumin and total bilirubin levels.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a confirmed diagnosis of hepatitis C, a viral infection affecting the liver, which likely dates back years.
Problem list: Hepatitis C; active; confirmed; unknown liver disease severity.
Plan:

I ordered a special ultrasound to determine the severity of the patient's liver disease and will also check for HCV RNA and HCV genotype to confirm the diagnosis and determine the best course of treatment.
I will have the nurse schedule an appointment for the patient's family members to undergo the same screening.
I will see the patient in about three weeks to review the results of the additional testing.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen in about three weeks to review the results of the additional testing.
